problem.title: Club of Young Aircraft Builders (easy version)

sources.notes: Statement and constraints: all entries are gaps; n,c <= 100, c <= m <= n*c, and total m over tests <= 1e4.
Official editorial page checked as the contest editorial; its tutorial body was lazy-loaded, so the recurrence was cross-checked with public writeups and self-derivation.
Used for the easy-version binomial DP recurrence over floors and total filled positions.
Used to cross-check the filtered-subsequence interpretation: among positions with value at least i, only the first c slots can receive value i.
Derived the validity condition from the launch rule: a value i may appear only before the c-th previous launch visible to floor i, and the top floor appears exactly c times.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution index checked; the substantive consulted text is recorded as the Luogu article source.
Official editorial submission link; source-code access was blocked by Codeforces browser checking, so it was not used for algorithm details.

annotation.summary: Count length-m floor sequences when every recorded launch is unknown, subject to the rule that a floor i resident can launch only while fewer than c previous launches from floors i..n have occurred, and by the end every resident has seen at least c launches.
annotation.constraints: n,c <= 100, c <= m <= n*c, all a_i = 0, and total m <= 1e4, so an O(n*m*c) counting DP with binomial coefficients fits.
annotation.core_idea: The final condition plus the launch rule implies exactly c launches from floor n. More generally, in the subsequence of positions whose values are at least i, all positions equal to i must lie among the first c positions of that subsequence. Process floors from high to low: after higher floors have occupied j positions, inserting k positions of the next lower floor only requires choosing which k of the first c visible slots of the new filtered subsequence are assigned to this floor, giving a factor C(c,k). This yields a binomial counting DP over the number of occupied positions.
annotation.complexity: O(n*m*c) time and O(m) or O(n*m) memory, plus binomial precomputation.
tricks: convert the launch rule into a first-c-slots condition on each filtered subsequence
process values from high to low so lower floors are inserted into a stable higher-floor skeleton
use binomial coefficients for choosing which visible quota slots receive the current value

solution_variants.name: high-to-low-visible-prefix-dp
solution_variants.summary: Start with c copies of floor n. For each lower floor, transition f[j] to f[j+k] by multiplying by C(c,k), because k occurrences of the current floor can occupy any k of the first c positions in the filtered subsequence of current-or-higher floors. The answer is the number of ways to reach m occupied positions.

solution_variants.name: deferred-phase-dp-all-gaps
solution_variants.summary: Equivalently scan phases by the current lowest floor that can still launch; when c active launches have accumulated, close one floor layer and choose by a binomial coefficient how many of those launches belong to that layer.

tags.evidence: The main algorithm counts partial assignments by how many sequence positions have been occupied after processing a prefix of floor values.
Every transition chooses a subset of the c visible quota slots to receive the current floor value, using C(c,k).
For each floor i, only the first c positions among values at least i may be assigned value i.
The solution is a modular counting argument over nested subsequences with binomial coefficients.
